text.title: technical-debt-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for technical-debt-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent scoring style/lint issues as "design debt".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Style and formatting are cosmetic, not structural. Inflating the register with cosmetic items buries real debt. Apply a severity rubric and reject purely stylistic signals at the classification stage.

failure-mode.description: Using LLM-generated time estimates ("2 days") without team calibration.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM effort estimates for debt paydown are confabulation; actual work is 5-10x more. Calibrate from past resolved-debt items in the register.

failure-mode.description: Agent proposing big-bang rewrites as the "fix approach" for debt items.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs bias toward rewrites. Prompt constraint: minimal local change, avoid diffs over 20 lines without explicit justification.

failure-mode.description: Agent auto-upgrading dependencies with semver-major version bumps.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Breaking changes in major upgrades require consumer testing. Route to humans and require breaking-change detection before any major upgrade is applied.
